skill.name: ai-ml
skill.description: Plan AI and machine learning projects across LLM apps, RAG, agents, ML pipelines, observability, and AI security.
skill.summary: Guides phased AI and ML delivery with specialist skill handoffs, prompts, and quality checklists.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown workflow text rather than executable code. The flagged backticks are inline skill names or fenced copy-paste prompts, and no shell execution, cryptographic implementation, reconnaissance command, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration was found. Residual risk is low because the workflow delegates work to other skills whose contents are outside this file.

low_findings.title: Static external command findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks and fenced prompt examples as Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines contain skill names and natural-language prompts, not executable commands or command interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown only, and the cited spans show lists or fenced prompt text. There is no script block, shell command, Ruby syntax, or user input execution path.

low_findings.title: Static weak cryptography findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged AI and ML workflow language as weak cryptography indicators. The cited lines describe project phases, architecture, RAG, and workflow metadata, with no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic algorithm names used as implementation guidance. The flagged locations are headings, descriptions, and workflow actions.

low_findings.title: Static system reconnaissance finding is a false positive
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged a skill name related to hybrid search as reconnaissance. The line is a recommendation to invoke a search implementation skill, not a command that enumerates host or network state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 90 is a Markdown list entry naming a related skill. It does not contain host inspection, network scanning, filesystem probing, or executable syntax.

low_findings.title: Workflow delegates to external skills
low_findings.description: The workflow instructs users to invoke many other skills. This is expected for a workflow bundle, but the safety of those separate skills is not proven by this file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The delegation pattern is explicit and benign in this file, but downstream skill behavior cannot be evaluated from this single Markdown document.

content.value_statement: AI projects can become fragmented across design, RAG, agents, MLOps, and observability. This skill organizes the work into phased prompts, checklists, and specialist skill handoffs.
actual_capabilities: Breaks AI delivery into design, LLM integration, RAG, agent, ML pipeline, observability, and security phases.
Lists specialist skills to invoke for each phase of an AI or ML project.
Provides copy-paste prompts for common AI engineering workflows.
Defines checklists for LLM integration, RAG systems, agents, observability, and release quality.
Connects AI work with related development, database, cloud, and testing workflow bundles.
limitations: Does not implement model code, infrastructure, or data pipelines by itself.
Depends on separate specialist skills that must be installed and reviewed independently.
Does not choose vendors, pricing plans, or compliance controls for a specific organization.
Does not validate model quality, retrieval accuracy, or production security automatically.

use_cases.title: Plan a new LLM product
use_cases.description: Use the phased workflow to define use cases, model choices, data flows, success metrics, and initial implementation prompts.

use_cases.title: Coordinate a RAG build
use_cases.description: Guide retrieval design, embedding selection, vector database setup, chunking, reranking, caching, and accuracy checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare AI operations
use_cases.description: Create a checklist for tracing, logging, evaluation, cost tracking, alerts, security controls, and release readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Map my AI project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new AI feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this AI/ML workflow to turn my product idea into phases, required skills, main decisions, and a first delivery checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Design an LLM application
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan an LLM application with provider selection, prompt templates, streaming, error handling, and token tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Build a RAG delivery plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to design a RAG implementation plan covering ingestion, chunking, embeddings, vector search, hybrid search, reranking, caching, and evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a production AI roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to create a production roadmap for agents, ML pipelines, observability, security controls, quality gates, and team handoffs.

output_examples.input: Plan a customer support assistant with retrieval.
output: Recommended phases: AI product design, LLM integration, RAG implementation, observability, and AI security.
Key decisions: support channels, model provider, knowledge sources, chunking strategy, vector database, evaluation set, and escalation rules.
Next prompt: ask the RAG engineering skill to design ingestion, retrieval, reranking, and caching.

output_examples.input: Prepare an AI agent prototype for an operations team.
output: Recommended phases: agent architecture, tool integration, memory design, orchestration, human review, and security controls.
Quality gates: tool permissions reviewed, errors handled, audit logging enabled, abuse monitoring defined, and observability configured.

output_examples.input: Move an ML model from notebook to production.
output: Recommended phases: ML pipeline design, data processing, training automation, evaluation, registry setup, deployment, monitoring, and alerts.
Specialist handoffs: ML engineering, MLOps, data engineering, AI evaluation, and cloud DevOps.

best_practices: Start with product goals, data flows, and success metrics before selecting models or frameworks.
Review each delegated specialist skill before using it in a production project.
Use the checklists as release gates for security, observability, testing, and documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the workflow as a replacement for architecture review or threat modeling.
Do not invoke downstream skills without checking their source, permissions, and instructions.
Do not deploy AI features before evaluation, monitoring, rate limits, and access controls are defined.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps plan LLM applications, RAG systems, AI agents, ML pipelines, observability, and AI security workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill write production code?
faq.answer: No. It organizes the work and points to specialist skills that can support implementation.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it require other skills?
faq.answer: It is most useful when the referenced specialist skills are available and separately reviewed.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown workflow guidance, with no executable code or confirmed dangerous pattern.

faq.question: What should teams verify before production use?
faq.answer: Teams should verify downstream skills, data handling, model behavior, security controls, evaluation quality, and observability coverage.
